content: When “ern” is added to north /nɔ:θ/ to form the adjective form, the pronunciation of the /θ/ becomes /ð/ in northern /`nɔ:ðən/. What kind of sound change takes place here?
The phenomenon in which two adjacent sounds influence one another and result in a single phoneme with some of the features of both sounds is known as _______.
A(n) ____ language is a language in which _____ can determine the meaning of a word.
Chinese is a(n) ________ language while English is a(n) ________ language.
In Chinese, _______ have relatively fixed tones.
Rising tone is most often used in _______.
A general rule of sentence accentuation is to stress_______ and weaken _______.
In English, there are three primary tones, fall, rise and fall-rise.
Vowel reduction is used on stressed syllables in order to make the unstressed syllables less prominent.
In English, each word (with very few exceptions) has only one primary stress and the stress is always on the vowel.
Chinese is a “syllable-timed” language while English is a “stress-timed” language.
The nucleus of an intonation unit is usually the first content word of the unit.
Speech rate should be speeded when there are fewer unstressed syllables within a rhythmic unit.
